## File size / split opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/features/agents/wake/wake_orchestrator.dart` (1225 lines) ‚Äî the only oversized impl file. Concrete split seams:
  - **Content-gating logic** (`_shouldSkipForAwaitingContent`, `setAwaitingContent`, `isAwaitingContent`, `_agentsAwaitingContent`, lines ~144‚Äì830) could become `WakeContentGate` (‚âà100 lines).
  - **Manual-wake enqueue** (`enqueueManualWake`, `restorePendingWake`, lines ~357‚Äì490) could sit in a thin `WakeEnqueuer` fa√ßade.
  - **`_drain` / `_executeJob` / `processNext`** (lines ~849‚Äì1192) are the dispatch kernel and could form `WakeDrainEngine` (‚âà340 lines), leaving the outer class as a thin coordinator.
  - After the split each file would be under 500 lines, and existing tests already cover each concern independently.
  **RESOLVED (adapted):** split via `part`-file extensions instead of new classes (so the orchestrator's instance state stays shared and `MockWakeOrchestrator` keeps intercepting the public API via a `processNext` delegator) ‚Äî `wake_batch_router.dart` (token routing + suppression + content gate, 342 lines) and `wake_drain_engine.dart` (drain/execute kernel, 416 lines); the coordinator class is 527 lines.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/agents/wake/wake_orchestrator_test.dart` (5659 lines) ‚Äî the mirror of the oversized impl. Concrete split:
  - One file per logical group that already exists in the source: `wake_orchestrator_subscription_test.dart`, `wake_orchestrator_drain_test.dart`, `wake_orchestrator_throttle_test.dart`, `wake_orchestrator_content_gate_test.dart`, `wake_orchestrator_abort_test.dart`, `wake_orchestrator_propagated_defer_test.dart`. The shared Glados generators and scenario classes in the first ~860 lines could move to a second helpers file (e.g. `wake_orchestrator_test_scenarios.dart`) since `wake_orchestrator_test_helpers.dart` already establishes this pattern.
  - Mirror-split the single Glados routing-scenario test (lines ~932‚Äì1018) into `wake_orchestrator_subscription_test.dart`; the drain-scenario Glados test (lines ~1541‚Äì1769) into `wake_orchestrator_drain_test.dart`; etc.
  **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the impl split used `part` files (one library), so the single mirror stays correct under the one-test-file-per-source rule; the shared scenario/generator helpers already live in `wake_orchestrator_test_helpers.dart`.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/wake/scheduled_wake_manager_test.dart` (958 lines) ‚Äî heavy Glados scenario boilerplate. The per-agent state setup helpers (`_GeneratedScheduledWakeSpec.toState`, lines ~87‚Äì130) could move to the shared `test/features/agents/test_utils.dart` so the file stays under 600 lines after the scenario classes are trimmed.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) ‚Äî the `_GeneratedScheduledWakeSpec`/scenario classes are single-consumer Glados generators specific to this file's properties; the repo convention keeps single-consumer helpers file-local (they already build on the shared `makeTestState`). At 958 lines the file is under the 1000 soft limit.

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[LOW]** `wake_orchestrator_test.dart:4071‚Äì4084` ‚Äî `AgentSubscription` constructor test at line 4071 checks `sub.id`, `sub.agentId`, `sub.matchEntityIds`, and `sub.predicate, isNotNull`. The `isNotNull` check for the predicate field is a near-smoke test; consider asserting the predicate's return value instead (`expect(sub.predicate!({'tok'}), isTrue)`). Low severity because the surrounding tests drive the predicate behaviour thoroughly.
  - **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî the test now uses a recording predicate (`return tokens.contains('e-1')`) and drives the stored function: `expect(sub.predicate!({'e-1'}), isTrue)`, `expect(sub.predicate!({'other'}), isFalse)`, and asserts the captured token sets, proving the exact function is stored and forwarded verbatim rather than merely non-null.

- [x] **[LOW]** `wake_orchestrator_test.dart:1854` ‚Äî `expect(capturedEntry.startedAt, isNotNull)` is a weak assertion for `startedAt`. A stronger check would be `expect(capturedEntry.startedAt, greaterThanOrEqualTo(DateTime(2024, 3, 15)))` to also confirm the clock was used correctly.
  - **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî the "persisted entry has correct fields" test is now wrapped in `withClock(Clock.fixed(DateTime(2024, 3, 15, 11)))` and asserts `expect(capturedEntry.startedAt, startedAt)` exactly (stronger than the suggested `greaterThanOrEqualTo`), since `_executeJob` stamps `startedAt: clock.now()`.

- [x] **[LOW]** `wake_orchestrator_test.dart:3454` ‚Äî `expect(writes.first.nextWakeAt, isNotNull)` only asserts presence; the nearby tests in the same group do check the exact deadline value, so this is consistent but could be tightened to assert the approximate deadline (e.g. within one second of `now + throttleWindow`).
  - **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî the "clears nextWakeAt when no follow-up job remains" test is now under `withClock(Clock.fixed(...))` and asserts the exact value `writes.first.nextWakeAt == now + throttleWindow` (direct match ‚Üí fast throttle), tighter than the within-one-second suggestion. The deferred-drain timer still fires under fakeAsync because its duration is `deadline.difference(clock.now())` = throttleWindow, which `emitAndDrain` advances by exactly.

- [x] **[MED]** `wake_orchestrator_test.dart` ‚Äî the four Glados scenarios (lines 932, 1541, 2688, 2849) each inline a full mock-stub block (‚âà20 lines of `when(...)` calls) that is nearly identical to the `setUp` block at line 867. After the proposed file split, these blocks should delegate to a shared helper (e.g. `_stubAllRepository(repo)`) to reduce copy-paste. The existing `restubWakeRunMethods` helper (`wake_orchestrator_test_helpers.dart:68`) already does part of this; extend it to cover `getAgentState` and `upsertEntity` as well.
  - **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî added `stubWakeRepositoryDefaults(repo)` (delegates to `restubWakeRunMethods` + no-op `getAgentState`/`upsertEntity`) used by the main `setUp` and the routing Glados; the two scenarios with verbatim no-op run-method stubs now call `restubWakeRunMethods`. The remaining scenario stubs are intentionally custom (they capture into per-scenario state) and cannot share a generic helper ‚Äî extending `restubWakeRunMethods` itself with state methods would overwrite them.

- [x] **[LOW]** `wake_orchestrator_test.dart` ‚Äî the two `group('enqueueManualWake', ...)` blocks (lines 2415 and 4175) share the same group name. The second one (line 4175, testing "clears pending subscription jobs") duplicates a concern already tested at line 2498. If these are intentionally distinct, rename the second group; if not, merge.
  - **RESOLVED:** renamed the second group to `'enqueueManualWake deferred-drain cleanup'`. The two are intentionally distinct: the first group's `'removes pending subscription jobs for the same agent'` asserts the manual job is the only one executed at manual-wake time, while the second test additionally elapses `throttleWindow` to prove the removed subscription job's deferred drain does NOT resurrect it ‚Äî a strictly additional invariant, so renaming (not merging) is correct.

- [x] **[MED]** `wake_orchestrator_test.dart` ‚Äî Tests at lines 1497‚Äì1519 (`start replaces previous subscription when called twice`) use real async (`async` test, not `fakeAsync`) with `pumpEventQueue()`. The comment explains why (`StreamSubscription.cancel()` on broadcast streams does not resolve within `fakeAsync.flushMicrotasks`). This is a legitimate exception documented in `test/README.md` ("Exception: Tests validating real I/O"). Flag for future investigation if the Dart team changes broadcast-stream cancellation semantics, but currently acceptable.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) ‚Äî as the item itself concludes: a documented, legitimate real-async exception per test/README.md; broadcast-stream cancellation does not resolve under fakeAsync.

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [x] **[LOW]** `WakeOrchestrator._preRegisterSuppression` + `_isPreRegisteredSuppressed` path in `_drain`: There is no standalone Glados property for "pre-registered suppression plus confirmed suppression produce the same aggregate coverage as their union". The existing drain-scenario Glados test covers this indirectly through the `_GeneratedWakeDrainScenario` model. No additional Glados test is needed ‚Äî the existing property exercises it with enough combinatorial depth.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) ‚Äî confirmed true: the item itself concludes no additional Glados test is needed; the drain-scenario Glados (`wake_orchestrator_test.dart`) exercises the pre-registered + confirmed suppression union with sufficient combinatorial depth.

- [x] **[LOW]** `RunKeyFactory.actionStableId` (lib:60‚Äì72) ‚Äî the JSON-canonicalization invariant (key-order-independent hash) is tested by a single Glados property in `run_key_factory_test.dart:441`. That test already covers the "same args, different key order ‚Üí same hash" and "different args ‚Üí different hash" invariants with 120 runs. No gap.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) ‚Äî confirmed true: `run_key_factory_test.dart` has a Glados property (line ~494, `numRuns: 180`, tagged 'glados') that reorders inputs to `actionStableId` and asserts an identical stable ID, plus example tests at lines 384/474 for the differs/key-order invariants. No gap.

- Overall: Glados coverage is extensive. The four Glados tests in `wake_orchestrator_test.dart` (routing/drain/pending-restore/post-run-throttle) plus the per-component tests in `wake_throttle_coordinator_test.dart`, `wake_suppression_tracker_test.dart`, `wake_runner_test.dart`, `wake_queue_test.dart`, `run_key_factory_test.dart`, and `scheduled_wake_manager_test.dart` amount to 13 distinct Glados test invocations covering every non-trivial pure invariant. No genuine gap found.

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[MED]** `wake_orchestrator.dart:619‚Äì629` ‚Äî the "escalate deadline when a fast-throttle match arrives during a propagated deferral" branch. The condition is `usesFastThrottle && deadline != null && immediate.isBefore(deadline)`. The existing test at line 3292 ("escalates deadline from morning to fast-throttle on direct match") covers the happy path. Missing: a test asserting that no escalation happens when `immediate.isAfter(deadline)` (i.e., the existing deadline is already sooner than `now + throttleWindow`). This is the `!immediate.isBefore(deadline)` branch ‚Äî currently covered only indirectly by the Glados throttle scenario which includes both orderings but does not assert the non-escalation in isolation.
  - **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî added `'a direct match during a sooner-armed deferral does not move the deadline'`: `restorePendingWake` arms a 30 s deadline + queued job (the restart-hydration shape), the direct match takes the merge path where the guard is evaluated in isolation (now+120s is NOT before 30 s ‚Üí no escalation), and the drain firing at the original 30 s deadline proves the deadline never moved. Note: the guard only protects the merge path ‚Äî a non-merged direct job re-arms now+120s unconditionally in the enqueue branch.

- [x] **[MED]** `wake_orchestrator.dart:416‚Äì432` ‚Äî `start()` guard: when `_notificationSub` is already non-null, it is cancelled before the new subscription is attached. The test at line 1497 (`start replaces previous subscription`) covers the "old stream is ignored" invariant but does NOT verify that the old subscription was actually cancelled (no `verify` on the old controller's listener count). A targeted assertion via `expect(controller1.hasListener, isFalse)` after the second `start` call would close this gap.
  - **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî the replaces-subscription test now asserts `controller1.hasListener` flips true‚Üífalse across the second `start` (and `controller2.hasListener` is true), proving the old subscription was actually cancelled rather than just ignored.

- [x] **[LOW]** `wake_orchestrator.dart:981‚Äì1014` ‚Äî `_executeJob` path where `insertWakeRun` throws: covered by the test at line 2190 ("catches insertWakeRun failure, releases lock, and continues drain"). That test verifies the drain continues but does NOT assert that the runner lock was released for the failing agent; it only calls `runner.isRunning('agent-1')` and `runner.isRunning('agent-2')` ‚Äî both of which are already checked at line 2218. Adequate; no gap.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) ‚Äî confirmed true: the `'catches insertWakeRun failure, releases lock, and continues drain'` test (now line ~2172) already asserts `runner.isRunning('agent-1')` is false (lock released) and the second agent's drain continues. The lock-release-for-failing-agent invariant is covered.

- [x] **[LOW]** `WakeRunner.runningAgentIds` stream (`wake_runner.dart:24`) ‚Äî the stream emitting the current running set after each acquire/release is not tested. The Glados scenario in `wake_runner_test.dart` tests `isRunning`, `tryAcquire`, and `release` state invariants but never subscribes to `runningAgentIds`. An example-based test asserting that the stream emits the correct set after acquire and again after release would improve observability coverage.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) ‚Äî already covered: `wake_runner_test.dart` has a dedicated `group('runningAgentIds stream')` (lines 465‚Äì540) with tests for emit-on-acquire, emit-on-release, cumulative multi-agent emission, no-emit-on-failed-acquire, and broadcast support; the Glados scenario also subscribes via `generatedRunner.runningAgentIds.listen` and asserts the full emission sequence. The observation predates these tests.

- [x] **[LOW]** `WakeRunner.activeStartedAtById` view (`wake_runner.dart:104`) ‚Äî `startedAt(agentId)` is tested implicitly through the abort/timeout tests in `wake_orchestrator_test.dart`, but `activeStartedAtById` (the unmodifiable map view) has no direct test. It is a trivial accessor backed by `UnmodifiableMapView`, so the risk is low.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) ‚Äî already covered: `wake_runner_test.dart` has `'activeStartedAtById exposes a live read-only view'` (line ~564) asserting the view is empty initially, the same instance reflects a new entry after acquire (`{'agent-1': fixed}`), and mutation throws `UnsupportedError`; the Glados scenario also equates `generatedRunner.activeStartedAtById` against the expected map every step.

- [x] **[LOW]** `WakeThrottleCoordinator._clearPersistedThrottle` double-check guards (`wake_throttle_coordinator.dart:141‚Äì145`) ‚Äî two `if (_throttleDeadlines.containsKey(agentId)) return` guards prevent a stale async write from clobbering a newly-set deadline. The Glados persistence scenario covers the "setDeadline then clear" interleaving but does not drive the precise race where a second `setDeadline` fires between the two guards. The risk is low because the guards are defensive, but an explicit fakeAsync test driving `clear + setDeadline + flushMicrotasks` in rapid succession would pin the invariant.
  - **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî added `'clearThrottle does not clobber a deadline re-armed during the persisted-clear getAgentState gap (second containsKey guard)'` to `wake_throttle_coordinator_test.dart`. It gates the clear's `getAgentState` on a `Completer`, injects a fresh `setDeadline` while the clear is suspended at the await, then completes the gate. Asserts: no `upsertEntity` write with `nextWakeAt == null` slipped through (guard 2 caught the re-armed deadline), the single persisted write carries the new deadline, and the in-memory `deadlineFor` survives ‚Äî pinning the second-guard invariant in isolation.

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/agents/wake/wake_orchestrator_test.dart:934,1543,2690,2851` ‚Äî Four Glados properties (`numRuns: 240`, `numRuns: 220`, `numRuns: 180`, `numRuns: 120`), totalling 760 Glados iterations in one file. Each iteration runs through the full `setUp` block at line 867 which wires ~15 `when(...)` stubs on `MockWakeRunner`, `MockWakeThrottleCoordinator`, `MockAgentRepository`, and others. Moving the mock construction to `setUpAll` and calling `reset(mock)` (or re-applying only the stubs that change per iteration) before each Glados run would eliminate ~755 redundant mock constructions ‚Äî the biggest single speed win in the wake directory. **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the premise doesn't hold on two counts. (1) `setUp` runs once per registered test ‚Äî a Glados `.test` is a single test whose body loops internally, so the group's ~15-stub setUp executes 4 times total, not 760. (2) All four property bodies construct their own per-iteration `generatedRepository`/`generatedQueue`/`generatedRunner` inside the body and never touch the setUp mocks; that per-iteration freshness is required because the bodies use `verifyNever`/`captureSingleWakeRun`-style per-iteration assertions ‚Äî moving mocks to `setUpAll` with `reset()` is the documented-unsound pattern for exactly this shape (stale stubs + cross-iteration interaction bleed).

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/wake/scheduled_wake_manager_test.dart:266,375` ‚Äî Two Glados properties (`numRuns: 180`, `numRuns: 160`). The quality section notes heavy Glados boilerplate. The per-iteration `setUp` rebuilds a `ScheduledWakeManager` with all stubs each time. Moving the manager and mock construction to `setUpAll` with a lightweight `reset(mock)` between iterations would cut 335 mock-build cycles.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) ‚Äî the proposal mismeasures the cost: mocktail mocks are plain in-memory objects (no I/O), `reset(mock)` clears stubs as well as interactions so per-case re-stubbing would remain, and sharing mock instances across generated cases risks interaction leakage between cases for no measurable gain.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/wake/wake_throttle_coordinator_test.dart:502,622` ‚Äî Two Glados properties (`numRuns: 180`, `numRuns: 180`). These test a pure state-machine (`WakeThrottleCoordinator`) backed by a `MockAgentRepository`. The coordinator is cheap to construct; the I/O stub cost per iteration is modest but still repeated 360 times. Shared `setUpAll` + `reset` would halve the number of coordinator instantiations.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) ‚Äî same reasoning as the manager item: per-case coordinator/mock construction is trivially cheap in-memory work; `reset` would force re-stubbing anyway and shared instances couple generated cases.

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/features/agents/wake/wake_queue_test.dart:409` ‚Äî Glados property at `numRuns: 160`. `WakeQueue` is a pure in-memory FIFO with no mocks; construction is trivial. The Glados loop here is already as fast as possible. No change needed.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) ‚Äî confirmed true: the queue Glados (line ~541, `numRuns: 160`, tagged 'glados') drives a pure in-memory FIFO with no mocks or I/O; nothing to speed up.

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/features/agents/wake/run_key_factory_test.dart:443`, `wake_runner_test.dart:100`, `wake_suppression_tracker_test.dart:154` ‚Äî Glados properties at `numRuns: 180`, `numRuns: 180`, `numRuns: 180` respectively. All three test pure data structures with no I/O or mock overhead; they are fast by design. No fake-time violations found anywhere in the wake suite.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) ‚Äî confirmed true: `run_key_factory_test.dart` (line ~494), `wake_runner_test.dart` (line ~98), and `wake_suppression_tracker_test.dart` (line ~152) are all `numRuns: 180`, tagged 'glados', over pure in-memory structures with no real I/O; fast by design.

---

## Summary

- `wake_orchestrator.dart` (1225 lines) is the **only oversized impl file** in this subdir; five other impl files are all comfortably under 200 lines.
- `wake_orchestrator_test.dart` (5659 lines) is the **only oversized test file** ‚Äî it is the largest single test file in scope and needs a mirror-split into at least 5‚Äì6 smaller files aligned with its existing `group(...)` structure.
- No fake-time violations (`Future.delayed`, `DateTime.now()`, `sleep`) found anywhere in the test suite.
- No inline mock class definitions ‚Äî all mocks correctly imported from `test/mocks/mocks.dart`.
- Glados coverage is thorough (13 Glados invocations across 7 test files); no genuine property-test gap identified.
- **Biggest opportunity:** split `wake_orchestrator_test.dart` into mirror-aligned files ‚Äî this is a maintenance burden today and a merge-conflict hot-spot on every future orchestrator change.
